q: Chemistry plays an essential role to achieve the ___ Sustainable Development Goals.

___ farming that uses fertilisers of organic origin such as compost manure, green manure, and bone meal instead of chemical fertilisers may be one way or another to minimise the environmental problems.
Instead of using chemical-based pesticides, plant-based pesticides that have minimum risks including ___ and insecticidal soaps can be used.
The ___ consist of insecticidal ingredients and they can be used as an insecticide.
To provide nutrients in a plant-available form, agrochemists have developed ___ technology to produce plant-based pesticides.
Plant-based pesticides significantly reduce ___ application rates.
Plant-based insecticidal soaps can be used to eliminate small ___ insects.
The municipal and non-hazardous industrial wastes have been converted to energy by ___ burning.
The process of converting wastes to energy by direct burning is known as ___ or pyrolysis.
In the process of pyrolysis, the organic content wastes in the reactor produce a mixture of hydrogen and ___.
Solar, wind, hydroelectric, biomass, biorefineries, geothermal, and ___ are important resources for future sustainable development.
Ethanol is the most important alcohol which can be produced by converting the sugar content of any ___ material into alcohol.
Productions of ethanol are from ___ materials such as corncob, cornstalk, cornhusk, sugarcane bagasse and sugarcane bark.
Biodiesel oil is produced from cultivated ___ oil.
Biodiesel oil is synthesised from plant oils by removing the by-product ___ molecule.
The combustion of fossil fuel generates ___ compounds whereas biodiesel does not.
The main purpose of wastewater treatment is to remove the various ___ that are present in the wastewater.
Photocatalysts binary metal oxides nanomaterials (e.g., $TiO_2, ZnO, Fe_2O_3$) are used to mineralise, decompose, and ___ the pollutants from wastewater.
Previously, ___ (TEL) was used as an anti-knocking agent.
Today, TEL is replaced by using ___ (MTBE).
___ are derived from renewable biomass sources such as corn starch, vegetable oils and fats.
Solvents like ___ ($Cl_2C=CCl_2$) used in dry cleaning of clothes, pollute the groundwater and are carcinogenic.
In the liquid $CO_2$ cleaning process, liquid carbon dioxide and other ___ cleaning materials replace tetrachloroethene.
Large amounts of ___ acid, $HOOC(CH_2)_4COOH$, are used each year for the production of nylon.
___ plating is the method of chromium plating most commonly known as chrome plating.
